title: 10 best herbal teas for eye twitching
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye twitching are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate the involuntary muscle spasms around the eyes. These teas often include natural ingredients known for their calming and soothing properties, such as chamomile, lavender, and valerian root. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, these herbal teas can help alleviate eye twitching and improve overall eye health. For instance, chamomile tea is widely recognized for its ability to soothe the nervous system, making it an ideal choice for those experiencing eye twitching due to anxiety or tension. Similarly, lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye irritation and inflammation, further contributing to the relief of eye twitching. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their quality of life, as they no longer have to deal with the discomfort and embarrassment caused by persistent eye twitching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage teas helps with eye twitching because of its calming and soothing properties. The tea, made from the flowers of the borage plant, contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce muscle spasms in the eyelid. This is especially helpful for people who experience frequent and severe eye twitching due to stress, anxiety, or allergies. By consuming borage teas regularly, individuals can promote relaxation and ease muscle tension, leading to a significant reduction in eye twitching episodes.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA is an anti-inflammatory fatty acid that helps reduce inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which may contribute to eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Borage roots contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm eye twitches.
Seeds: Borage seeds are rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that helps reduce inflammation and promote relaxation to alleviate eye twitching symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borage leaves and flowers in early morning or evening when dew is present.
Rinse the harvested parts gently with filtered water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Combine one teaspoon of dried borage leaves and flowers or two teaspoons of fresh borage with 8 ounces boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving hot or pouring over ice, enjoying within 24 hours preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with eye twitching because it contains flavonoids, which are potent antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and calm the eyelid muscles, reducing spasms and twitching. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of pot marigold can help to relieve stress and anxiety, common triggers of eye twitching. By drinking this herbal tea, individuals may experience a reduction in frequency and severity of eye twitches.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Marigold's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to eye twitching by calming down irritated tissues.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyelid muscles, which could be a contributing factor to eye twitching.
Calming effects: Marigold has been traditionally used as a nervine herb, meaning it can help soothe and calm the nervous system, which may help alleviate eye twitching caused by anxiety or stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing eye twitching caused by inflammation.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing relief from twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers from your garden or store in an airtight container for later use.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or three teaspoons of fresh flowers per cup of boiling water.
Heat filtered water to a boil and pour it over the measured calendula flowers in a teapot or mug.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired tea strength and flavor profile.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with eye twitching because of its ability to soothe and calm the eyes. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce puffiness and redness, which can contribute to twitching. Additionally, eyebright contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can cause muscle spasms in the eyes. By promoting relaxation and reducing irritation, eyebright teas can effectively alleviate eye twitching and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Eyebright's astringent property helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which can soothe irritated eyes and relax twitching muscles.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Eyebright tea can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with eye twitching, promoting healing and comfort.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in Eyebright tea help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye twitching and other eye issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the eyebright plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Bark: The bark of the eyebright plant contains antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes, alleviating eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers or three-quarters teaspoon dried.
Combine the herbs with one cup boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Serve the cooled tea hot or at room temperature and enjoy within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with eye twitching because it contains a calming compound called linalool, which has a soothing effect on the nervous system. When consumed as a tea, linalool is absorbed into the bloodstream and can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of eye twitching. The tea's calming properties can also help to relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing muscle spasms that may be contributing to the twitching. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, English lavender teas can provide relief from pesky eye twitches.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Herbal English Lavender tea can help relieve eye twitching by calming muscle spasms in the eyes, face, and head, which are often the underlying cause of twitching.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of lavender tea may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to twitching.
Anxiolytic: Lavender tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that can exacerbate eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them effective in reducing eye twitching.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a compound called linalyl acetate, which has a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce stress-related eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender flowers and rinse them gently under cool running water.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried lavender flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or until it reaches your preferred strength and color.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the lavender solids.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy the calming aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with eye twitching because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension. The tea's antispasmodic properties help to relax the eyelid muscles, thereby reducing or eliminating unwanted twitches. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing effects can also alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often underlying causes of eye twitching. By promoting relaxation and calming the nerves, herbal lemon balm teas provide a natural remedy for alleviating eye twitching discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Lemon balm tea has antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax and calm spasms in the muscles around the eyes, reducing or eliminating twitching.
Anti-anxiety and stress-reducing: Lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate eye twitching caused by anxiety or stress-related muscle contractions.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in lemon balm tea may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress around the eyes, which could contribute to twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain a higher concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce eye twitching.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and relaxing the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemon balm leaves or dried lemon balm tea bags from trusted sources.
Measure two teaspoons of dried lemon balm or one tablespoon of fresh lemon balm into a tea infuser.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 195°f to 205°f in a teapot.
Steep the lemon balm leaves or tea bag in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids, allowing it to cool for serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with eye twitching because it contains Passiflora incarnata, a natural relaxant that calms the nervous system. The herb's sedative properties help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for eye twitching. Additionally, Maypop tea may also help to relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and twitching. By promoting relaxation and calmness, Maypop tea can provide relief from eye twitching and promote overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Passiflora incarnata (Passionflower) content: This property helps to calm anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for eye twitching, by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.
Tryptophan: As an amino acid precursor to serotonin and melatonin, tryptophan can help regulate sleep patterns, reduce stress, and alleviate eye twitching symptoms.

Antioxidant compounds (e.g., flavonoids, phenolic acids): These compounds help protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to eye twitching and related vision problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Bark: Containing natural astringents, the bark helps constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried maypop flowers and leaves in the morning.
Combine one tablespoon of dried maypop herb with one cup boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids.
Serve the warm tea immediately, allowing it to cool slightly before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with eye twitching because its calming properties soothe the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate twitching. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also help to alleviate dryness and irritation in the eyes, which is a common trigger for twitches. Additionally, herbal large-leaved lime teas contain antioxidants that promote healthy tear production and lubrication, further reducing the likelihood of eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Large-leaved lime tea's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm muscle spasms in the eyelid, reducing the frequency and intensity of eye twitching.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in large-leaved lime tea can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which may be contributing to the eye twitching.
Antioxidant properties: Large-leaved lime tea's antioxidants can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to muscle spasms and twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the large-leaved lime plant are used to make teas that help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing twitching and spasms.
Bark: The bark of the plant is used to make teas that calm inflammation and irritation in the eyes, alleviating itchiness and redness associated with eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried large-leaved lime leaves, use one teaspoon of dried or three teaspoons of fresh material.
Combine the leaves with one liter of boiling water, steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discard solids and reserve liquid.
Sweeten the tea to taste with honey if desired, stir well to dissolve the sweetener.
Serve the herbal tea hot or chilled, garnish with fresh lime leaves for added flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with eye twitching because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce muscle spasms in the eyelid. The herb's active compounds, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work to promote relaxation and ease tension, which can help to alleviate symptoms of eye twitching. As the tea helps to calm the nerves, it can also reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for eye twitching, providing relief from this frustrating and sometimes irritating condition.
remedy_properties: Sedative Properties: Valerian tea's sedative properties can help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and twitching associated with eye strain or fatigue.
Antioxidant Properties: Valerian tea's antioxidants can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which may contribute to eye twitching, and promote overall ocular health.

Muscle Relaxant Properties: Valerian tea's muscle relaxant properties can help alleviate spasms and cramps in the muscles around the eyes, including those that control eye movement, reducing twitching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are a primary source of valerenic acid, which helps to calm the nervous system and relieve eye twitching symptoms.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes contain sesquiterpenes that have a sedative effect on the body, making them effective in reducing eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots or rhizomes of mature plants with a subtle sweet aroma.
Wash the roots or rhizomes gently with filtered water and pat them dry to remove impurities.
Chop the cleaned roots or rhizomes into small pieces using scissors or mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Combine 1 teaspoon of chopped valerian root or rhizome with 250ml boiling water in a teapot and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding solids and serving hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena teas helps with eye twitching because of its calming and soothing properties. The tea's flavonoids, such as verbascoside and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms in the eyes and face. When consumed regularly, verbena tea can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers of eye twitching. Additionally, the tea's antispasmodic properties can directly calm twitching muscles around the eyes, providing relief from this annoying and distracting condition.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Verbena tea's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for eye twitching.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Verbena tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye twitching.
Muscle relaxant properties: Verbena tea contains compounds that can help relax muscle spasms, including those affecting the eyelid muscles, which can cause eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Verbena roots have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to soothe and relax the muscles that contribute to eye twitching.
Bark: The bark of the verbena plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating twitching and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves at dawn or evening when essential oils are most potent.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 24-48 hours before storage.
Measure two teaspoons of dried verbena officinalis leaves and add to a large teapot with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 7-10 minutes or until the tea reaches desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discard solids, and serve hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jujube teas helps with eye twitching because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that help to soothe and calm the muscles around the eyes. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce redness and irritation, allowing the eyes to relax and recover from any underlying tension or stress that may be contributing to the twitching. Additionally, jujube teas' natural sedative effects can promote a sense of calm and relaxation, further helping to alleviate eye twitching symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Jujube tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to eye twitching by calming down irritated muscles and tissues around the eyes.

Adaptogenic properties: Jujube tea's adaptogens, such as ginseng and licorice root, help balance the body's response to stress, reducing anxiety and fatigue that may be exacerbating eye twitching symptoms.

Calming and sedative properties: The soothing compounds in jujube tea, like flavonoids and saponins, can calm the nervous system, relaxing muscles around the eyes and helping to reduce spasms and twitches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the jujube plant are used to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms, making them effective in treating eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the jujube plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated eyes and reduce twitching caused by dryness or inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 2-3 grams of dried jujube fruits in cold water to remove any impurities.
Combine the rinsed jujubes with 1 liter of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 15 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding solids.
Let the tea cool and settle before serving hot or chilled, with optional sweeteners or flavorings added to taste.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye twitching with herbal teas is by using chamomile or valerian root tea. Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea, then gently apply it as a compress to the affected eye. These teas have natural properties that can help relax the muscles around the eye and reduce twitching. For best results, use organically grown herbs and drink the tea yourself to aid in relaxation. However, if twitching persists or becomes severe, consult a healthcare professional as it could indicate a more serious condition.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye twitching that are treated with herbal teas include nervousness, anxiety, and stress. Herbal teas such as chamomile, valerian root, and passionflower are known for their calming properties and can help alleviate symptoms. Chamomile tea is often used for its anti-inflammatory and relaxant effects, while valerian root tea is known for its sedative properties, which can promote relaxation and reduce eye twitching caused by stress or anxiety. Passionflower tea is also used to treat anxiety and restlessness, and can help alleviate eye twitching caused by these conditions. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedies.
